summary: One surgical edit to strengthen the entry bridge from Chapter 3. The Chapter 3 ending has Dan memorizing the case designation K-4471; Chapter 4's glossary passage at line 95 already referenced 'coincident bilateral node' but omitted the case number. Added K-4471 alongside it and adjusted 'words' to 'terms' to accommodate both phrases and alphanumeric designations. Opening-construction diversity verified (walking/domestic scaffold vs. Chapter 5's sensory/spatial office opening — distinct). Exit seam to Chapter 5 is clean: the domestic quiet of Theo drawing contrasts with the institutional hum of the conference table. No other edits needed — entry/exit continuity, character voice, dialogue register, and contraction levels are all consistent with the style bible and boundary context.
edits: Line 95: Added 'and *K-4471*' to Dan's expanding glossary of dehumanizing labels, bridging to Chapter 3's ending where he committed the case number to memory; changed 'words' to 'terms' to encompass both phrases and designations
